original: Player: Emily
It was the grave of Emily Grant—[p/]such a shame that she died so young.
I briefly paid my respects.
It was the grave that Eris was attending to earlier.
The name on the stone said “Emily Grant”,[p/] and her lifetime appears to be awfully short.
She must have only been twelve years old when she died.
Player: Grave
It was a grave for an individual that I didn't know.
Out of respect,[p/] it'd be best to leave it undisturbed.
Player: Back
Player: The Trial
ERIS: That was my first time watching a court trial.
ERIS: You were truly remarkable.
TYRION: ...Thank you.[p/] Were you in the gallery?
ERIS: I’ve always enjoyed watching physical battles [p/]in the theatre.
ERIS: But I see now that battles of wits can be just as entertaining to watch.
TYRION: “Entertaining”?
CELESTE: Yeah,[p/] I know what you mean.
CELESTE: You see the odds are stacked against him,[p/] but then he suddenly pulls out this crazy deduction that takes the whole trial back.
TYRION: I’ve...[p/] never really thought of it like that...
ERIS: But I’m sure it’s far more entertaining to be the one [i]making[/i] the deductions.
TYRION: I’m...[p/] not really sure what you mean.
TYRION: I practice law so that I can help those persecuted by the justice system.
TYRION: I don’t really do it to "entertain" myself.
ERIS: But is that really true?
ERIS: Don’t you find the experience even the least bit entertaining, Tyrion?
TYRION: Um...[p/] I wouldn’t really put it that way.
ERIS: You’re far too modest.[p/] You save people from the clutches of death.
ERIS: To your clients,[p/] you might as well be a knight in shining armor.
Eris: ...
Eris: I know you're looking for it,[p/] but apparently those new operating systems don't like it when I peek at your real name.
Eris: I'm all for a bit of fun,[p/] but that's not worth blue-screening people.
Tyrion: ...What?
Eris: Don't worry about it.
Player: Illusion Magic
TYRION: Earlier,[p/] you said that you were studying [b]illusion[/b] magic.
ERIS: I am.[p/] Did you want to ask me about the prosecution’s theory?
TYRION: ...Yes, actually.[p/] How did you know?
ERIS: Disguising an undead with an illusion is definitely possible.
ERIS: As Lady Steelwind said,[p/] [b]Illusory Disguise[/b] would be the best spell to use for that.
TYRION: What if someone created a freestanding illusion?
TYRION: We know that Wallace didn’t animate and disguise the professor’s corpse.
TYRION: So,[p/] what if the illusion wasn’t disguising [i]anything[/i]?
TYRION: Maybe someone created a freestanding illusion of Bellwether walking around?
ERIS: For something like that,[p/] one would probably use [b]Major Illusion[/b].
Player: Major Illusion
TYRION: Hm...
CELESTE: What is it?
TYRION: I don't think [b]Major Illusion[/b] is what we’re looking for.
TYRION: Do you remember what Jan said?
JAN: I was cleaning a mess in the hallways.
JAN: Bellwether knocked over a vase...[p/] And there were shards and dirt everywhere.
TYRION: If the “Bellwether” that Jan saw was a Major Illusion,[p/] then she couldn’t have knocked over that vase.
TYRION: Illusions created by that spell don’t have any mass.
ERIS: I see.[p/] How perceptive of you.
CELESTE: Aren’t there any other [b]illusion[/b] spells that have mass?
ERIS: No.[p/] All known [b]illusion[/b] spells in Wyverngarde can only recreate visual and auditory sensations.
ERIS: None of those spells can create something that someone can touch.
CELESTE: So that means that someone disguised[p/] [i]something[/i] to make it look like Bellwether.
Celeste: ...
ERIS: Why the look of suspicion, Celeste?
CELESTE: S-[p/][s/]Sorry,[p/][s/] I wasn’t-!
ERIS: Calm yourself.[p/] I was only joking.
ERIS: I don’t blame you for considering every possibility.
ERIS: In fact,[p/] I’m flattered you think I’m capable of pulling off such an elaborate ruse.
CELESTE: ...
TYRION: ...Anyways,[p/] this [i]does[/i] tell us one important thing.
ERIS: What’s that?
TYRION: The “Bellwether” that Jan saw had a physical mass to it.
TYRION: It wasn’t a freestanding illusion.[p/] It was someone disguised as her.
Player: Gravestone
ERIS: Hm...[p/] It’s quite a shame, isn’t it?
Eris looked pensively towards the gravestone.
The name on the stone said “Emily Grant”,[p/] and her lifetime appeared to have been awfully short.
TYRION: S-[p/]Sorry for interrupting you...[p/] Was she someone you knew?
ERIS: I’m actually here on Wallace’s behalf.
CELESTE: Wallace knew her?
ERIS: Wallace joined the academy as part of a program run by Headmaster Redmond.
ERIS: At the time,[p/] the headmaster was reaching out to mages among the commonfolk.
ERIS: He had started a program to admit them to the academy through a scholarship program.
ERIS: Both Emily and Wallace were among those mages.
CELESTE: Wow...[p/] That must have been an amazing opportunity for them.
ERIS: The truth is,[p/] neither of them actually cared about learning magic.
ERIS: The real problem was that Emily had a deadly illness.
ERIS: She and Wallace were poor orphans,[p/] and they obviously didn’t have the resources to treat it.
ERIS: But if they enrolled in the academy...
TYRION: The academy would be obligated to give her medical treatment.
ERIS: The Imperial Academy teaches the best mages in the kingdom.
ERIS: They thought that curing her illness would be an easy task for the academy’s healers.
ERIS: ...But they were mistaken, I’m afraid.
ERIS: I’m sure you know this by now,[p/] but healing spells can only treat wounds.[p/] They can’t cure illnesses.
ERIS: Emily’s condition only got worse over the years,[p/] and eventually,[p/] she succumbed to her illness.
ERIS: Wallace was furious.[p/] He didn’t understand why the kingdom was outlawing magic that could save lives.
TYRION: ...And that’s why he decided to learn it himself.
ERIS: Yes.[p/] The headmaster was inspired by Wallace’s resolve.
ERIS: He gave him access to the academy's forbidden tomes of [b]necromancy[/b].
ERIS: And with the headmaster's guidance,[p/] Wallace learned the spells that he knows today.
CELESTE: So, that’s why...
ERIS: It was supposed to stay a secret between him and the headmaster.
ERIS: ...But I’m sure you know how that turned out.
Player: BACK
Player: Runestone
Eris: Seriously?[p/] You're showing me the badge now?
Tyrion: "Badge"?[p/] It's an Attorney's Runestone.
Eris: We're really threading the line, aren't we?
Eris: Are you going to make a habit of showing this thing to everyone you meet?
Tyrion: I-[p/][s/]I just think it's neat.
Player: Tyrion
Eris: I wasn't quite sure what to make of you at first,[p/] but you are truly remarkable.
Tyrion: Thanks...[p/] I think?
Eris: I quite enjoyed watching you battle Lady Steelwind.
Eris: I only hope the climax lives up to my expectations.
Player: Celeste
Eris: That's a fancy-looking sword, Celeste.
Eris: Is it from the Eastern Kingdoms?
Celeste: Y-[p/]Yes, actually.
Celeste: How did you know?
Eris: I've encountered a few weapons from there before.
Eris: They have a unique style that's easy to recognize.
Player: Aria
Eris: Lady Steelwind is really something.
Tyrion: Yeah.[p/] I feel like she's always one step ahead of me.
Eris: But she [i]does[/i] have a habit of relenting when she realizes she's wrong.
Eris: I think you deserve a worthier adversary.
Tyrion: I mean...[p/] She can be a pain sometimes,[p/] but I'm glad that she pursues justice even if it means she loses.
It was strange...[p/] I would've never seen myself holding such high respect for a noble.
Eris: Anyways,[p/] I wonder what would drive a noble like her into becoming a lowly prosecutor.
Eris: I'd love to know the story behind that.
Player: Eris
Tyrion: We know that Wallace was accepted through Redmond's program...
Tyrion: But what about you, Eris?
Eris: Are you trying to ask me if I'm a noble bastard?
Tyrion: S-[p/]Sorry, I didn't mean-
Eris: No need to apologize.
Eris: Although,[p/] the answer to that question is a bit...[p/] complicated.
Eris: My family is quite wealthy, but I wouldn't consider them a part of the nobility.
Hm...[p/] Her family must've been part of a guild or something.
Eris: I would say it's more like several circles.
Player: Catherine
Eris: Catherine Bellwether wasn't the best person.
Eris: She constantly used her authority to belittle and torment people.
Eris: I'm sure the world is better off without her in it.
Tyrion: T-[p/]That's extremely blunt,[p/] Eris...
But I couldn't exactly disagree with her.
Eris: I have no interest in indulging those in power who are unworthy of it.
Player: Wallace
Eris: It's strange, really.
Eris: If it weren't for his disposition,[p/] Wallace would be quite the person to fear.
Tyrion: What do you mean?
Eris: It's clear to the three of [i]us[/i] that he didn't commit the murder.
Eris: But think about how easily he [i]could[/i] have done what he was accused of.
Eris: He could instantly kill anyone using [b]Necrotic Blight[/b],[p/] and he could even raise them as undead using [b]Animate Corpse[/b].
Tyrion: But Wallace would never do something like that.
Eris: [i]*sigh*[/i][p/] I agree.
Eris: But one can often be surprised by what someone can do if they are pushed far enough.
Eris: It really makes me wonder how idiotic the people who bully him are.
I'd rather not think about that.
Player: Theodore
Tyrion: You were with the headmaster in his office earlier.
Tyrion: Are you two acquaintances?
Eris: In a way, yes.[p/] You could say that I’m a patron of his.
Eris: I’m interested in why he would go so far as to include the commonfolk in the academy.
Eris: His generosity can clearly be a vice sometimes.
Eris: Enrolling commonfolk mages into the academy was already a controversial move.
Eris: But teaching one of them [b]necromancy[/b] illegally...
Eris: Even [i]if[/i] Wallace is acquitted,[p/] I don't see things getting easier for him.
Default
Tyrion: What do you think about this, Eris?
Eris: Really, now?
Eris: I can't tell you [i]everything[/i] you need to get through this case.
Eris: What would be the fun in that?
Tyrion: Um...[p/] Okay?
CELESTE: Tyrion...?[p/] What are we doing here?
TYRION: I’m...[p/] not sure.
For some reason,[p/] I felt drawn to this place.
I was supposed to make my way back to the Academy,[p/] but I suddenly found myself here.
It was strange,[p/] but I couldn't even remember how we'd arrived here.
CELESTE: Wait...[p/][s/] Is that Eris?!
Celeste pointed further into the cemetery.[p/] Eris was standing there in front of a gravestone.
She was placing flowers on it and appeared to be chanting a short prayer.
CELESTE: Well...[p/] We found her.
TYRION: We...[p/] should probably talk to her later.
TYRION: It would be rude to disturb her now.
As we were about to leave,[p/] Eris suddenly turned to face us.
She was far away,[p/] but she'd clearly noticed us and was beckoning us over.
ERIS: Oh, hello there.[p/] Were you two looking for me?
TYRION: S-[p/]Sorry.[p/] We [i]were[/i] looking for you,[p/] but we understand if you’d rather talk later...
ERIS: No.[p/] I was just finishing up here.
ERIS: Did you have something you wanted to talk to me about?
TYRION: Well...[p/] We wanted to ask you some questions about the case.
CELESTE: Oh, Tyrion![p/] I almost forgot!
Celeste: Let me show you one of the new spells I learned.
CELESTE: ...[b]REPERIO ARCANUM[/b]!
ARIA: ...!
TYRION: W-[p/][s/]What was that?
CELESTE: [i]That[/i] is the incantation for the [b]Detect Magic[/b] spell.
TYRION: [b]Detect Magic[/b]...?[p/] Wait...[p/][s/] Does that mean—?
CELESTE: Yep![p/][s/] I can see magical traces while we're EXAMINING crime scenes!
Player: Painting
Tyrion: It's a painting of some sort of musician.
Tyrion: There's some text written below it.
Tyrion: "Doctor Nicolas Goussu:[p/] Inventor of the Arcane Engine."
Tyrion: "His work revolutionized trade and travel across the seas."
Tyrion: "He was well known for not discriminating between the commonfolk and the nobility,[p/] judging his assistants based purely on their merit."
Tyrion: "When he wasn't conducting research within the Imperial Academy,[p/] he was known to compose symphonies in his free time."
It sounded like he was a man of many talents.
Player: Body
Tyrion: There's a chalk outline where the professor's body was.
Tyrion: It appears that most of this blood came from her abdomen.
Tyrion: Is that where the fatal wound was?
ARIA: ...
TYRION: There’s a lot of blood in this puddle...[p/] but there’s no trail leading to it.
TYRION: And there aren't any magical traces anywhere else in the room.
Tyrion: I doubt the murderer used magic to clean it.
CELESTE: Does that mean that the murderer attacked her in this room?
TYRION: Maybe...[p/] But it’s hard to say for certain.
Player: Window
Tyrion: The lecture hall has a window.
Celeste: I feel like I'd just stare outside all day if I had to sit in this place.
Celeste: How long do they have to sit here, anyway?
Aria: Lectures here last for an hour at a minimum.
Celeste: An [i]hour[/i]?![p/] No wonder all the nobles are such psychopaths!
Aria: Present company excluded, I hope.
Celeste: Yeah.[p/] You're not that bad, Aria.
Aria: ...How have you learned so many spells if you're so distractible?
Celeste: I don't know.[p/] I just look at the spell formula and...[p/] do it.
Aria: I...[p/] see.
Celeste: ...Is that not normal?
Player: Books
Celeste: Oh![p/] They have books here too.
Tyrion: These appear to be textbooks about alchemy.
Celeste: Aw...[p/] Lame.
Player: Students
Tyrion: This must be where the students sit during lectures.
Celeste: Ugh...[p/] I can't imagine sitting in one place all day.
Tyrion: Have you never attended a school before?
Celeste: Do you seriously think a dump like Kornhaven has standardized education?
Celeste: I learned from books that I...
She glanced at Steelwind before finishing that thought.
Celeste: ...I learned from books that I [i]appropriated[/i] from some traveling merchants.
Celeste: What about you?
Tyrion: ...I attended a public school when I was younger,[p/] but Ms. Tymora handled most of my education herself.
Celeste: Hm...
Player: Detect Body
There was a trace of [b]necromancy[/b] on the body.
According to Steelwind,[p/] that was the primary piece of evidence implicating Wallace.
CELESTE: ...What?
TYRION: What’s wrong?
CELESTE: There’s a trace of [b]necromancy[/b] in Bellwether's blood.
TYRION: [b]Necromancy[/b]?
CELESTE: Isn’t [b]necromancy[/b] the type of magic that creates zombies?
CELESTE: She’s not just going to get up and walk away, right?
ARIA: ...We’re honestly not sure.[p/] The Inquisition is on edge about that as well.
TYRION: But how does that implicate Wallace?
Aria: Oh,[p/] he didn't tell you?
ARIA: Wallace Nightgrave is a necromancer.
ARIA: We believe that he drained her life force using a spell called [b]Necrotic Blight[/b].
Aria: Spells like that tend to interfere with the [b]divination[/b] spell we use to identify the cause of death.
ARIA: However,[p/] there were no visible wounds.[p/] Only a [b]necromancy[/b] spell could have killed her.
Tyrion: ...Do you have a copy of the Autopsy Report?
Aria: Of course,[p/] but it's incomplete because of the aforementioned reasons.
ARIA: This whole ordeal has put everyone on edge.
Aria: This is the first case of necrotic homicide we've had in nearly a century.
I couldn't believe it...[p/] I'd only read about [b]necromancy[/b] in fantasy novels.
I never imagined that I'd end up defending a necromancer.
TYRION: But if what you're saying is true,[p/] weren’t there plenty of other mages who could use [b]necromancy[/b]?
Tyrion: This [i]is[/i] an academy for mages, after all.
ARIA: No.[p/] The knowledge required to learn [b]necromancy[/b] is heavily controlled.
Aria: Mr. Nightgrave already violated several laws simply by learning these spells.
ARIA: I'm baffled as to how he even got access to that knowledge in the first place.
Right...[p/] [b]Necromancy[/b] sounded like some dark magic—[p/]even by the nobility's standards.
I'd be terrified to know why [i]anyone[/i] would want to learn that type of magic.
ARIA: The fact that he was the only known student who knew these spells makes him our only suspect. [h="But it’s still too convenient...|Something else is happening..." /]
TYRION: ...!
I seldom caught thoughts from Steelwind,[p/] and they usually only appeared when someone brought up her father.
She was usually very composed,[p/] and I still didn't see a hint of that uneasiness in her posture.
Normally,[p/] I could only see thoughts when the target was stressed or excited...
[i]Something[/i] about this case must've been rattling her.
TYRION: ...Is there something you’re not telling us, Steelwind?
TYRION: ...
The Eye couldn't catch any more thoughts from her.
Player: Questions for us
ARIA: Ms. McCoy.[p/] It’s my understanding that you were adopted.
ARIA: Would you happen to know who your biological parents are?
Celeste tensed and shifted uncomfortably.
TYRION: ...That's kind of personal, isn't it?
ARIA: You agreed to answer my questions.
TYRION: I [i]did[/i],[p/] but I didn’t think you meant—
CELESTE: I-[p/][s/]It’s fine, Tyrion.[p/] It's for Wallace.
She took a breath.[p/] This had always been a sore subject for her.
CELESTE: I don’t know who my birth parents are.[p/] I’ve never tried looking for them.
ARIA: ...I see.
ARIA: If I recall correctly,[p/] your [b]Spell Compendium[/b] only contained one spell.
ARIA: How did you learn [b]Mage Blade[/b] in the first place?
CELESTE: I bought a scroll from a merchant a few years ago.
Celeste: It had [b]Mage Blade's[/b] spell formula on it.
Aria: But surely someone taught you how to cast it.
Celeste: No, I'm self-taught.
Steelwind's eyes narrowed.[p/] She was being unusually interrogative about this.
Aria: ...You can't teach yourself magic, Ms. McCoy.[p/] That's not possible.
Celeste: Well,[p/] [i]I[/i] did.
Aria: ...Are you sure you're not mistaken?
CELESTE: Hey![p/] I've got a system, okay?
Celeste: I even learned a few new spells today.
Aria: I’m sorry...[p/] “spells”?[p/] As in plural?
Celeste: Yeah![p/] [i]I'd[/i] like to think I'm pretty good at this.
Aria: ...How long did it take you to learn these spells?
CELESTE: A few hours, I think.
ARIA: ... [h="She taught herself multiple spell formulas|within a few hours?!" /]
ARIA: I...[p/] see.
Aria: I think that’s all I need to ask for now.[p/] Thank you, Ms. McCoy.
CELESTE: Um...[p/] Okay?
Steelwind looked like she was deep in thought.[p/] Why was she so interested in Celeste's magic?
Player: The Murder
TYRION: Do you know what happened here, Steelwind?
ARIA: Well,[p/] the City Watch received a call from the academy.
ARIA: They reported an attack on campus grounds and put the entire building on lockdown.
ARIA: The city watch set up a perimeter around the building while they waited for the system to deactivate.
TYRION: They had to wait?[p/] Why didn't the academy just deactivate the system manually?
ARIA: I’m not sure about the specifics,[p/] but supposedly,[p/] the lockdown can’t be shut down once triggered.[p/] It's a safety mechanism.
ARIA: You’ll have to ask one of the academy’s admins if you want to know more.
ARIA: From what I've heard,[p/] you two were also present during the lockdown.
ARIA: Did either of you see anything suspicious?
CELESTE: No,[p/] but we actually met the professor that morning.
CELESTE: She was obviously still alive then.
ARIA: I see...[p/] That [i]does[/i] match with the accounts from our other witnesses.
Aria: She must have gone to her meeting with the headmaster afterward.
Tyrion: She had a meeting with the headmaster?[p/] How long did this go on for?
Aria: From 12:00 PM to 1:00 PM—[p/]about an hour before her death.
If the headmaster met with Bellwether,[p/] he might've known where she went after the meeting.
TYRION: Would you happen to have a map of the academy?
ARIA: I do.[p/] I'll transfer it to your Runestone.
TYRION: There’s only one direct path from the headmaster’s office to this lecture hall.
Why would she even enter the lecture hall in the first place?
Did she die here,[p/] or was she murdered somewhere else?
Rancid Smell
CELESTE: Ugh...[p/] That smell is driving me crazy.
CELESTE: What [i]is[/i] that?
ARIA: There was a bit of an accident during an alchemy lecture a few days ago.
ARIA: The instructor misread the directions for an experiment.
ARIA: As a result,[p/] he accidentally produced this rancid-smelling vapor.
TYRION: Vapor...?[p/] It’s not toxic, is it?
ARIA: I wouldn’t be standing here if it were.
ARIA: ...It’s just extremely unpleasant to the senses.
Aria: They temporarily closed this lecture hall until they could get rid of the smell.
Aria: House Steelwind actually...[p/] [i]used[/i] a similar concoction during the Great Luminite War.
Celeste: See?[p/] This is why I stick to [b]transmutation[/b] magic.
Aria: Ms. McCoy.[p/] Alchemy is a [i]branch[/i] of [b]transmutation[/b].
Celeste: What?!
Celeste: I could've been making potions this entire time...
How was the body discovered?
TYRION: How was the body discovered?
ARIA: Two members of the kitchen staff spotted her body inside this classroom.
ARIA: They reported the situation to the campus admin while they attempted to save her life.
ARIA: But it was too late.[p/] The professor had already passed away long before any healers arrived.
TYRION: Those two staff members...[p/] Who were they?
ARIA: Valentina and Valerie Ianus.[p/] They’re sisters.
TYRION: How are they holding up?[p/] I imagine they'd be rattled after discovering the body of their coworker.
ARIA: From what Orym said,[p/] they were extremely tense during questioning,[p/] but I suppose that's to be expected.
ARIA: He told me that they couldn't sit still.
Aria: He found it difficult to take their statements because of that.
Tyrion: They "couldn't sit still"?
ARIA: Well,[p/] he's always making excuses for his incompetence.
Why is Wallace a Suspect?
TYRION: We’ve already told the Inquisition this,[p/] but Wallace was with us the entire time.[p/] He has a perfect alibi.
ARIA: Strong, maybe,[p/] but his alibi is far from perfect.
ARIA: Ms. McCoy herself told us that.[p/] There was a small time period when she wasn't with him.
TYRION: ...What?
CELESTE: Right...[p/] It was after we came to check on you when you knocked over that shelf.
CELESTE: We got separated,[p/] and I kind of got...[p/] lost in the fiction section.
Aria: She found an early release copy of [i]Deaths at Daggerfall[/i].[p/] She binge-read it for over an hour.
Celeste: Don't just rat me out to him![p/][s/] He's my boss!
Aria: Nonsense.[p/] They're well-written books.[p/] No one would fault you for that.
I mean—[p/]I [i]might[/i] fault her for that.
Celeste: Wait...[p/] You read them too?
Aria: Y-[p/][s/]Yes...[p/] But—
Celeste: What did you think about the ending of "The Uncrowned"?!
Tyrion: ...Celeste?
Aria: I found the cliffhanger to be needlessly unsatisfying.
Aria: They're stringing the readers along to sell more copies of the sequel.
Celeste: Oh, come on![p/][s/] Anticipation just makes each new book that much more fulfilling.
Tyrion: Celeste.
Aria: I eat brunch with the author occasionally.[p/] The fame has clearly gotten to her head.
Aria: She's clearly just dragging her feet to raise profits.
Celeste: You eat brunch with Altheris Duskweaver?!
I cleared my throat.
Tyrion: Everyone![p/] We are [i]all[/i] supposed to be working right now.
Celeste: You're such a killjoy sometimes.
Aria: N-[p/][s/]No...[p/] He's right.[p/] I don't know what got into me.
Celeste: Well,[p/] after this case is over,[p/] I'm tagging along for brunch with you.
Aria: What...?
Celeste: Hey,[p/] you tried to convict me for murdering my dad.
Celeste: You owe me, Steelwind.
Aria: [i]*sigh*[/i][p/] I suppose you have a point.
Aria: Ah,[p/] an Attorney's Runestone.[p/] I have one of these myself.
Tyrion: They're useful, aren't they?[p/] The note-taking and picture functions save so much time during these investigations.
Aria: They're really a marvel of artificery.[p/] You'll see a lot of these among the nobility.
Tyrion: ...Why would nobles need to store evidence?
Aria: I-[p/][s/]I—
Aria: They have other uses, Mr. Cuthbert.
Aria: I wasn't expecting to meet you here, Mr. Cuthbert.
Aria: The Imperial Academy isn't exactly open to outsiders.
Tyrion: We originally came here to do some research,[p/] but I never expected to get wrapped up in another homicide...
Aria: ...What kind of research were you doing?
Celeste: Don't bother asking.[p/] He won't tell you.
Aria: I'm glad to see you under less...[p/] dire circumstances, Ms. McCoy.
Aria: Are you accompanying Mr. Cuthbert on his investigation?
Celeste: Yeah.[p/] I'm working as Tyrion's bodyguard now.
Aria: I see.[p/] Well, you certainly have the credentials for it.
Aria: Although,[p/] I'm surprised he's able to afford your services.
Tyrion: ...What's [i]that[/i] supposed to mean?
Aria: I meant no offense,[p/] but work like this [i]does[/i] appear to be below Ms. McCoy's pay grade.
Celeste: Yeah.[p/] I was surprised, too.[p/] Ruby pays me a lot to protect this guy.
Aria: Ah,[p/] Ruby Tymora is paying you.
Aria: ...I suppose that makes more sense.
Player: Aria|Orym
